description: Konami's gothic action-platformer franchise spanning NES to DS. Follows the Belmont clan and their eternal war against Dracula. Invented the Metroidvania genre with Symphony of the Night (1997).

media.title: Castlevania
media.description: Simon Belmont storms Dracula's castle. The original. Short, brutal, and iconic.

media.title: Castlevania II: Simon's Quest
media.description: Open-world RPG hybrid sequel. Simon must gather Dracula's body parts to break a curse.
media.notes: Core: fceumm. Use a guide — in-game hints are mistranslated.

media.title: Castlevania III: Dracula's Curse
media.description: Trevor Belmont, Sypha, Alucard, and Grant fight Dracula. Branching paths and multiple characters. Chronologically the first game in the story.

media.title: Super Castlevania IV
media.description: Retelling of the original Castlevania with 8-directional whip and vastly improved controls. The best classic-style entry.

media.title: Castlevania: Bloodlines
media.description: WWI setting. Genesis exclusive. Two playable characters — John Morris and Eric Lecarde.

media.title: Castlevania: Dracula X
media.description: SNES port of Rondo of Blood. Richter Belmont's era (1792). Inferior to the PC Engine original but mobile-playable.

media.title: Castlevania: Symphony of the Night
media.description: Alucard explores Dracula's inverted castle. Invented the Metroidvania genre. The series masterpiece and best entry point for new players.

media.title: Castlevania Legends
media.description: Sonia Belmont's story. The only Game Boy Castlevania. Officially decanonised by Koji Igarashi.

media.title: Castlevania: Circle of the Moon
media.description: GBA launch title. Nathan Graves uses the DSS card combo system. Dark atmosphere, high difficulty.

media.title: Castlevania: Harmony of Dissonance
media.description: Juste Belmont (Simon's grandson) searches for a missing friend. The weakest GBA entry — easy and visually cluttered.

media.title: Castlevania: Lament of Innocence
media.description: Leon Belmont forges the Vampire Killer whip. Origin of the Belmont bloodline. Story-essential lore with repetitive 3D gameplay.
media.notes: PCSX2 required. Optional — save until invested in the series.

media.title: Castlevania: Aria of Sorrow
media.description: Soma Cruz discovers he may be Dracula's reincarnation. Soul Steal mechanic. Outstanding story twist. Best GBA entry.

media.title: Castlevania: Curse of Darkness
media.description: Hector hunts Dracula's remnants 3 years after CV3. Connects to the anime S3–S4 storyline.

media.title: Castlevania: Dawn of Sorrow
media.description: Direct sequel to Aria of Sorrow. Soma Cruz faces a cult seeking to revive Dracula. Play Aria first — Dawn spoils its twist immediately.

media.title: Castlevania: Portrait of Ruin
media.description: WWII era. Jonathan Morris (son of Bloodlines' John Morris) and Charlotte fight through painting worlds. Dual-protagonist system.

media.title: Castlevania: Order of Ecclesia
media.description: Shanoa of the Ecclesia order seeks the Dominus seal. Glyph absorption system. The hardest and best DS entry.

media.title: Castlevania
media.description: Trevor Belmont, Sypha Belnades, and Alucard fight to stop Dracula's war on humanity. Directly adapts the CV3 era. S1 = 4 eps, S2 = 8 eps.
media.notes: Netflix. Watch after Castlevania III or Symphony of the Night.

media.title: Castlevania (S3–S4)
media.description: Hector and Isaac's arcs in the aftermath of Dracula's death. S3 = 10 eps, S4 = 10 eps.
media.notes: Netflix. Watch after Curse of Darkness for the Hector payoff.

media.title: Castlevania: Nocturne
media.description: Richter Belmont fights during the French Revolution (~1792). Set before Symphony of the Night. S1 = 8 eps, S2 = 8 eps.
media.notes: Netflix. Watch after Symphony of the Night.
